summary: Implemented Sign in with Google and removed Apple login. Backend: added GoogleLoginRequest DTO, a newUser flag on LoginResponse, a new GoogleAuthService that verifies the Google ID token via Google's tokeninfo endpoint and finds-or-creates the user (AuthProvider=GOOGLE, role User, placeholder phone, random unusable password), a POST /api/auth/google endpoint in AuthController, and app.google.client-id config. Frontend: added googleLogin to authService, loaded Google Identity Services and rendered the official Google button (with a demo/fallback button when NEXT_PUBLIC_GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID is unset), removed the Apple button, and routed brand-new Google accounts through the existing Buyer/Seller role-selection screen. Updated vi/en i18n (removed apple, added continueWithGoogle and googleNotConfigured). Backend compiles; no frontend lint errors. Next step: configure GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID env (backend) and NEXT_PUBLIC_GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID (frontend) with a real Google OAuth client to enable the live button.
current_status: Google login feature complete and compiling end-to-end; awaiting real Google OAuth client id configuration for production use. Role selection (User vs Seller) preserved for both email and Google sign-up.
